candidate_summary: Activation-thermography for LLMs: per-neuron basin-stability map predicting prompt-injection vulnerability via counterfactual probing.

results.title: Large Language Models for Anomaly and Out-of-Distribution Detection
results.rationale: Survey of LLM anomaly/OOD detection; attention heatmaps and token-importance are mentioned but the candidate's per-neuron basin-stability framing for prompt-injection prediction is not present. Mildly thematic overlap (anomaly = hotspot analogy) but neither counterfactual probing of decision-basin boundaries nor prompt-injection vulnerability mapping is covered.

results.title: AD-LLM: Benchmarking Large Language Models for Anomaly Detection
results.rationale: Benchmark for LLM text anomaly detection (using LLMs as detectors on NLP tasks), not internal neuron interpretability or jailbreak vulnerability prediction. No overlap with the basin-stability / activation-patching axis.

results.title: LLM-LADE: log anomaly detection with explanation
results.rationale: Log anomaly detection via PEFT and post-hoc explanation generation. No mechanistic interpretability, no prompt-injection focus, no decision-basin reasoning.

results.title: Exploring LLM-based Frameworks for Fault Diagnosis
results.rationale: Uses LLMs as agents for industrial fault diagnosis. Reverses the candidate's direction (LLM diagnosing faults rather than LLM internals being diagnosed). No counterfactual probing of LLM neurons.

results.title: Hybrid LLM-Assisted Fault Diagnosis for 5G/6G Networks
results.rationale: Network fault diagnosis with hybrid pipeline. Domain-mismatched: no LLM mechanistic interpretability and no security/jailbreak vulnerability prediction.

results.title: Boosting Anomaly Detection With LLMs
results.rationale: Blog-level survey of LLM anomaly augmentation. Mentions activation-based interpretability in passing but no per-neuron basin-stability for prompt-injection. Not a substantive match.

results.title: LLMAD: Time Series Anomaly Detection with LLMs (KDD 2025)
results.rationale: In-context time-series anomaly detection; LLM used as detector on external series, not interpreted internally. No prompt-injection or counterfactual-probing content.

results.title: Thermal fault diagnosis of complex electrical equipment based on infrared image recognition
results.rationale: Pure source-domain paper (industrial infrared imaging of electrical equipment). No LLM content at all; one keyword overlap is incidental.

verdict_independent: PASS — no surfaced result clears the hit threshold. The proposed combination of (a) per-neuron basin-stability scoring from dynamical-systems theory, (b) counterfactual prompt perturbation, and (c) prompt-injection vulnerability prediction is not directly present in these 8 results.

disagreement_details.note: Primary's judge=0.69 sits just below threshold; I score lower because the survey covers attention heatmaps for OOD/anomaly detection rather than the specific per-neuron basin-stability or prompt-injection vulnerability mapping. Hit verdict still agrees (both = miss).

honest_note: Reflecting on prior knowledge: activation patching and counterfactual probing for LLM safety are well-established (e.g., circuit analysis of refusal, mechanistic anatomy of jailbreaks, refusal-direction ablation in Arditi et al.-style work, and probing for harmful-instruction features). The DYNAMICAL-SYSTEMS basin-stability formalism (Menck et al. 2013) applied per-neuron to predict prompt-injection vulnerability is, to my knowledge, not yet a published framing as of late 2026 — though closely related notions (steering vectors, robustness manifolds, jailbreak directions, loss-landscape sharpness for adversarial vulnerability) exist. Given that, I would set a low-to-moderate judge score for the 8 surfaced results: none of them touch the mechanistic-interpretability-of-jailbreaks axis at all (they are anomaly/fault detection papers plus the source thermography paper). The primary's judge=0.69 on the NAACL anomaly-detection survey looks slightly inflated to me — the survey is about LLMs DETECTING anomalies, not about probing LLMs' own neurons for vulnerability. I therefore agree with the primary's PASS verdict but on a stricter basis. A genuinely confounding result would be a 2024-2026 paper combining activation patching + counterfactual probes + prompt-injection prediction (e.g., from Anthropic interpretability, Apollo Research, or a mechanistic-jailbreak paper). The current search queries did not surface that literature, so the PASS reflects search-coverage limits as much as genuine novelty.
